items.headline: City Council addresses affordable housing protections when subsidies end
items.summary: The council is establishing new rules to protect affordability restrictions on housing when project-based subsidy contracts expire or properties face foreclosure. This affects how long affordable units stay affordable after initial subsidy periods end.

items.headline: San José updates storm sewer master plan with new consultant agreement
items.summary: The city is amending its agreement with Wood Rodgers, Inc. to continue city-wide master planning for the storm sewer system. This planning guides future infrastructure improvements to handle flooding and stormwater.

items.headline: Comm Hill Phase 3.1 approved: 5 new homes on Altino Boulevard
items.summary: San José is moving forward with Phase 3.1 of the Comm Hill development, adding five single-family homes ranging from $487K to $573K along Altino Boulevard. This represents continued residential growth in the area with diverse floor plan options (Types 2a-R through 4c).

items.headline: Sherly Place development adds 3 new homes near downtown
items.summary: Three new single-family homes approved at Sherly Place with prices between $496K and $570K. The project's 100% BEMP (Building Energy Efficiency Standards) designation indicates these homes will meet high energy efficiency standards.

items.headline: City Council reviews FY 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program
items.summary: Mountain View City Council held a study session to examine the Capital Improvement Program for fiscal year 2026-27, which outlines planned infrastructure and facility projects. This is worth following to understand what physical improvements—such as roads, parks, buildings, or utilities—the city plans to invest in over the next year.

items.headline: Sunnyvale Proposes Changes to Tenant Protections Ordinance
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance amending Chapter 19.71 of the Municipal Code, which governs the Residential Tenant Protections Program. This change could affect rent control, eviction protections, or other tenant rights for Sunnyvale renters.

items.headline: City releases Children and Youth Services Master Plan status update
items.summary: The council reviewed progress on the Children and Youth Services Master Plan, along with annual reports on youth programs including the Safe Summer Initiative and youth intervention services. This shows how the city is investing in youth programs and services.

items.headline: Downtown building seeks historic status upgrade and 5,000 sq ft bonus
items.summary: A property at 405 Kipling Street is requesting elevation from Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource status, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area as an incentive for seismic and historic rehabilitation. This affects downtown development potential and could signal how the city balances historic preservation with density.
